text.title: Acceptance Criteria Quality Rubric Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Acceptance Criteria Quality Rubric. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: AC is testable — there is a binary check that proves it true/false.
rule.rationale: AC is testable — there is a binary check that proves it true/false.

rule.statement: AC is atomic — one assertion per criterion; 'and' splits the AC.
rule.rationale: AC is atomic — one assertion per criterion; 'and' splits the AC.

rule.statement: AC describes observable behaviour, not internal implementation.
rule.rationale: AC describes observable behaviour, not internal implementation.

rule.statement: Negative / error cases included alongside happy path.
rule.rationale: Negative / error cases included alongside happy path.

rule.statement: AC scoring <4/5 is rejected and rewritten.
rule.rationale: AC scoring <4/5 is rejected and rewritten.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-ac-quality-rubric.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
